revision.content:
Put your ideas about the Bloominglabs Booth here!

* Photo booth
** Jay is willing to bring his photo booth (doesn't necessarily have to be in the Bloominglabs area)

* Laser booth
** Dan (a.k.a. LaserDan)

* Vacuum forming machine
** Need a volunteer for this - we'll pull one from the volunteer pool
** Need to work with the Soapy Soap people to mold soap from the molds we make at Makevention
** Need to have things to mold 
** Plastic sheets have arrived and are at Bloominglabs, 2x .020"x40"x72" white styrene sheets, 2x .030"40"x72" white styrene sheets

* Sales/donation desk
** Need a volunteer for this

* DIY speakers desk 
** Need a volunteer for this
** Rowan said that he can help out for a few hours here
** The Library may want to do these at their booth

* Twinkie launcher
** Need a volunteer for this - we'll pull one from the volunteer pool
** We now have two twinkie launchers!
** Need to acquire twinkies
** Need to test foot pump

* Soldering irons
** Adafruit kits?

* Heath's booth
** Bicycle - currently being built.
*** Really want it on the bike trail for people to ride.
*** I'd be glad to switch out with people throughout the day.
** Rain barrels 
*** If someone else wants to run the rain barrel booth that would be awesome-tastic.
*** Could have some barrels there that people could build them on site.
*** a sign up sheet for a Bloominglabs barrel class.
*** another sign up sheet for people to purchase rain barrels pre-made.

* Nick's generator bike

* Workshop signups
** If anyone is interested in leading a workshop in the next few months it would be a great idea to have a signup sheet for the public in our booth. This could also be a trial to see what workshops the public would be interested in if you have been thinking of doing something for a while. It would be most useful to post specific dates, but just a signup sheet would be better than nothing.
** Arduino workshop
** Machining workshop: mill and lathe machine tools, a 4 week course (tentatively available to the public Jan 2014). 
** Using PGP encryption with email: a short history of encryption followed by use of PGP/GPG with email. This would also be an install session where attendees bring their laptops and we do an install, share keys, and send encrypted messages.

* T-shirts - are printed and ink should be set now. dosman will distribute volunteer shirts

* MAME cabinet

* <strike>Smoke cannons</strike>
** <strike>We'd need Jonathan's smoke bombs for this</strike>
***Joe Stone has a smoke machine if that makes the Smoke Cannons more feasible
* <strike>Circuit bending booth</strike>
* <strike>Frankentstein stuffed animal booth</strike>
